title: Travelers Risk Index Reveals Work-Related Pressures May Contribute to Distracted Driving
text: HARTFORD, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) today announced the results of the 2022 Travelers Risk Index on distracted driving, revealing that dangerous behaviors continue on U.S. roadways. Despite one-third of respondents stating that their own driving has improved over the past couple years, most admitted to making phone calls and reading text messages while behind the wheel. “This year’s findings expose a gap between people’s perception that they can drive safely while using their smartphone and the National Highway Traffic Safety Administration data, which shows a 20% increase in car crashes and fatalities since the pre-pandemic levels of 2019,” said Michael Klein, Executive Vice President and President of Personal Insurance at Travelers. “We all play a role in helping to address this public health crisis; it’s imperative that we simply silence our phones while driving and stay focused behind the wheel.” The Travelers Risk Index results suggest that work-related pressure might lead to distracted driving. The majority of business managers (86%) expect employees to respond to work-related communications at least sometimes while outside the office during work hours. One-third expect employees to answer or participate in work calls while driving. Such expectations may contribute to the 42% of respondents who say they take work-related calls, texts or emails while driving. When asked why, 43% of those respondents state that it might be a work-related emergency, 39% feel they always need to be available, and 19% say their boss will be upset if they don’t answer. Distracted drivers are more likely to correct their behavior when passengers speak up, according to the Travelers Risk Index. Eighty-seven percent of respondents who use a phone while driving said they would be less likely to do so if a passenger mentioned it. However, the likelihood of passengers speaking up was dependent on who the driver was. For instance: 44% of respondents would speak up to a friend or family member if they were driving distracted. Only 11% would speak up to a coworker. Just 7% would say something to their rideshare driver. The use of driver assistance technologies in vehicles is further complicating the issue of driver distraction, a topic that will be discussed at Travelers Institute’s virtual event today at 1 p.m. EDT. To learn more about the event, visit the Travelers Institute website. For more information about the Travelers Risk Index and ways to reduce distracted driving, please visit Travelers.com. About the Travelers Risk Index In January 2022, Hart Research conducted a national online survey of 1,000 consumers, ages 18 to 69, regarding their perceptions and behaviors related to distracted driving. Separately, Hart surveyed 1,000 executives from businesses of all sizes. Both surveys were commissioned by Travelers. About Travelers The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) is a leading provider of property casualty insurance for auto, home and business. A component of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Travelers has approximately 30,000 employees and generated revenues of approximately $35 billion in 2021. For more information, visit Travelers.com.

title: Providence Hires New Chief Legal Officer
text: Anna Newsom joins Providence as executive vice president and chief legal officer RENTON, Wash., March 28, 2022 /PRNewswire/ -- Providence, one of the largest health systems in the nation, announced today it has named Anna Newsom as its new executive vice president and chief legal officer.

title: Travelers Institute Announces Spring Webinar Lineup
text: HARTFORD, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Travelers Institute, the public policy division of The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV), today announced the upcoming sessions of the Wednesdays with Woodward® series. The webinars will explore a variety of topics, including investigating insurance fraud, the state of the COVID-19 pandemic, a behind-the-scenes look at the Travelers Championship golf tournament, and a deep dive into telematics and usage-based insurance. “Our programs will continue to focus on the trends and market dynamics that are shaping the current outlook for businesses,” said Joan Woodward, President of the Travelers Institute and Executive Vice President of Public Policy at Travelers. “We look forward to providing our agents, brokers and customers with expert insights into the challenges and opportunities that impact their lives both at work and at home.” Following is the schedule of upcoming webinars: March 30: “Ready to Take Over? Driver Distraction in the Age of Automation” – Insurance Institute for Highway Safety (IIHS) research scientists Dr. Ian Reagan and Dr. Alexandra Mueller will discuss the ongoing danger of distracted driving on our roadways and share insights into IIHS’ new safety ratings for partially automated vehicles, which assess how well these vehicles help drivers maintain focus on the road. April 6: “60 Minutes in Personal Insurance: Opportunities in a Changing Marketplace” – Travelers leaders Michael Klein, Executive Vice President and President of Personal Insurance, and Loree Toedman, Senior Vice President of Personal Insurance Field Sales, will discuss the rapidly evolving personal lines marketplace and how insurance carriers and agents can adapt and respond most effectively. April 13: “Level Up Your LinkedIn Presence to Grow Your Business, Brand and Network” – Sulemaan Ahmed, Co-founder and Principal Educator at Servo Annex, and Brian Tietje, Global Client Director for LinkedIn Corporation, will join Woodward to offer best practices and strategies for optimizing your LinkedIn profile, social selling and business development for individuals and organizations. April 20: “The Future of Fighting Insurance Crime” – David J. Glawe, President and CEO of the National Insurance Crime Bureau, and Nick Seminara, Executive Vice President and Chief Claim Officer at Travelers, will provide a behind-the-scenes look at the latest methods their teams use to root out insurance fraud and protect consumers. Glawe added, “Insurance crime causes billions of dollars in economic harm to the nation every year. Technological advancements and globalization have ushered in a new wave of threats, ranging from cyber criminals to climate change and infectious diseases. Better data analytics integration and timely dissemination of actionable intelligence products are necessary to confront the evolving schemes of organized criminal networks.” May 4: “Former U.S. Surgeon General Jerome Adams on Lessons Learned from the Front Lines of the Pandemic” – Dr. Adams will share his expertise related to the COVID-19 pandemic. Topics will include what our “new normal” could look like, updates on vaccines and boosters, and his latest work on expanding health equity. May 11: “Behind the Scenes at the Travelers Championship” – Andy Bessette, Executive Vice President and Chief Administrative Officer at Travelers, and Nathan Grube, Tournament Director of the Travelers Championship, will take the webinar audience behind the scenes of the Travelers Championship, including a virtual tour of the TPC River Highlands clubhouse. May 25: “How’s My Driving? Telematics and the Insurance Experience” – Peter Gulbrandsen, Vice President and National Auto Lead for Personal Insurance at Travelers, and Ryan McMahon, Vice President of Strategy at Cambridge Mobile Telematics, will offer an inside look at the evolution of usage-based insurance, including discussions of driving trends during the pandemic, changing customer utilization of telematics offerings, and the safety benefits and insights that these programs can provide. June 1: “Storm Warning: Managing the Risk of Increasing Hurricane Catastrophes” – Eric Nelson, Senior Vice President of Enterprise Catastrophe Risk Management at Travelers, joins leading authorities in hurricane forecasting and risk mitigation to discuss the ways insurance professionals and their customers can prepare for the 2022 hurricane season. June 8: “Hacked! What’s Your Plan?” – Charlie Platt, Senior Director of Forensics – Incident Response – Solutions for Arete, and Tim Francis, Vice President and Enterprise Cyber Lead at Travelers, will discuss practical tips and key considerations for businesses when putting together a cyber incident response plan. June 29: “The Exponential Rise of Nuclear Verdicts” – Harold Kim, Chief Legal Officer and Executive Vice President of the U.S. Chamber of Commerce and President of the Institute for Legal Reform, and Alexia Cruz, Senior Vice President and Claim General Counsel at Travelers, will discuss the rising trend of extreme jury awards – known as “nuclear verdicts” – and how these decisions are impacting the insurance industry and commerce alike. “Nuclear verdicts are a problematic trend that affect almost every industry, leading to more lawsuits and skyrocketing premiums,” said Kim. “I’m looking forward to discussing what’s driving the increases in jury verdicts and what defense counsel can do to counter this trend.” The series features expert guest speakers who will join Woodward to discuss critical issues that individuals and businesses face. All events take place at 1 p.m. ET and are free and open to the public. Online registration and past-event replays are available on the Travelers Institute website. About the Travelers Institute The Travelers Institute®, the public policy division of The Travelers Companies, Inc., engages in discussion and analysis of public policy topics of importance to the insurance marketplace and to the financial services industry more broadly. The Travelers Institute draws upon the industry expertise of Travelers’ senior management as well as the technical expertise of many of Travelers’ underwriters, risk managers and other experts to provide information, analysis and solutions to public policymakers and regulators. Travelers is a leading provider of property casualty insurance for auto, home and business. For more information, visit Travelers.com.

title: Travelers Announces 2022 Personal Insurance Agent of the Year Award Winners
text: HARTFORD, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) today announced the 2022 recipients of its Personal Insurance Agent of the Year Award. The honorees were recognized for their excellent customer service, dedication to their communities, commitment to Travelers and long-term profitable growth. “We are fortunate to partner with thousands of outstanding agents, but this particular group of individuals has distinguished themselves by always putting the needs of our customers first,” said Michael Klein, Executive Vice President and President of Personal Insurance at Travelers. “We appreciate their dedication and commend them for their achievements. We look forward to continuing and growing these successful relationships.” The following are the 2022 Agent of the Year Award honorees: Gordy Bunch, TWFG Insurance Services Robb Frees, Frees Insurance Holly Herron, Assure Alliance, Inc. Bruce Humphrey, HomeServices Insurance John Mahoney, Weichert Insurance Agency Jim Mignone, ML Cutler & Co. Jeffrey Mongeon, Polly Insurance Agency Lisa A. Pouchak, Ten Eyck Group Karl D. Susman, Susman Insurance Agency Steven Tanner, Tanner Insurance Service Ltd Youngdon Yun, Premier Group Insurance About Travelers The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) is a leading provider of property casualty insurance for auto, home and business. A component of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Travelers has approximately 30,000 employees and generated revenues of approximately $35 billion in 2021. For more information, visit Travelers.com.

title: Is Travelers Stock Fairly Priced?
text: Travelers' stock has gained approximately 15% YTD as compared to the 9% drop in the S&P500 index over the same period. Further, at its current price of $179 per share, it is trading just above its fair value of $178 – Trefis' estimate for Travelers' valuation.

title: Travelers Institute to Host Former U.S. Secretary of Defense Chuck Hagel for a Discussion of Geopolitical Risks
text: HARTFORD, Conn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Travelers Institute, the public policy division of The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV), will host a webinar on Wednesday, March 16, titled “Global Hot Spots and Geopolitical Risks with Former U.S. Secretary of Defense Chuck Hagel.” The event will explore how diplomatic and security challenges can potentially impact the business community. “Secretary Hagel is a leading voice in international relations and has shaped foreign policy and geopolitical discourse throughout his career – from his two terms as a Republican U.S. senator to his tenure as secretary of defense under President Barack Obama,” said Joan Woodward, President of the Travelers Institute and Executive Vice President of Public Policy at Travelers. “We are pleased to have him share his expertise on how the global issues we face may affect businesses of all sizes.” Woodward will moderate the session, which will take place at 1 p.m. ET. Secretary Hagel will provide his perspective on current events impacting the United States, including the conflict in Ukraine, U.S.-China relations, the supply chain crisis, cybersecurity and the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic. Hagel added, “We are living at the most dangerous time in the world since World War II – a defining time.” The session, which is part of the Travelers Institute® Wednesdays with Woodward® series, is free and open to the public. Anyone interested in registering can sign up here. To learn more about the Travelers Institute, please visit Travelers.com/Travelers-Institute. About the Travelers Institute The Travelers Institute, the public policy division of The Travelers Companies, Inc., engages in discussion and analysis of public policy topics of importance to the insurance marketplace and to the financial services industry more broadly. The Travelers Institute draws upon the industry expertise of Travelers’ senior management as well as the technical expertise of many of Travelers’ underwriters, risk managers and other experts to provide information, analysis and solutions to public policymakers and regulators. Travelers is a leading provider of property casualty insurance for auto, home and business. For more information, visit Travelers.com.

title: Travelers Schedules Conference Call to Review First Quarter 2022 Results
text: NEW YORK--(BUSINESS WIRE)--The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) will review its first quarter 2022 results at 9 a.m. ET on Tuesday, April 19, following the release of results earlier that morning. Investors can access the call via webcast at investor.travelers.com and by dialing 844-895-1976 within the United States or 647-689-5389 outside the United States. A slide presentation, statistical supplement and live audio broadcast will be available on the same website. Following the event, replays will be available via webcast for one year at investor.travelers.com and by telephone for 30 days by dialing 800-585-8367 within the United States or 416-621-4642 outside the United States. All callers should use conference ID 1450817. About Travelers The Travelers Companies, Inc. (NYSE: TRV) is a leading provider of property casualty insurance for auto, home and business. A component of the Dow Jones Industrial Average, Travelers has approximately 30,000 employees and generated revenues of approximately $35 billion in 2021. For more information, visit Travelers.com.
